person.name: John de Comyn
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
John was named by the Lord Lyon as the eldest son of Robert de Comyn. However, no sources are given for this relationship and it has been called into question by more recent historians such as Alan Young.

Comyn married (Unknown) Giffard, a daughter and co-heiress of Adam Giffard of Fonthill. 

«b»There were four sons from this marriage:«/b»
1.) William Comyn, d. 1144

2.) Richard Comyn, d. bef. 1182

3.) Walter Comyn

4.) Osbert Comyn, d. 1144

John Comyn was killed during the wars between Queen Maud and King Stephen, sometime after 1135.

«b»Research Notes«/b»
Sir James Balfour Paul inserts an additional generation here, i.e. he states that John had only one son, William, and William in turn had three sons: William, Richard, and Walter. More recent scholarship refutes this, assigning William, Richard, Walter, and Osbert all as the sons of John Comyn. Since William and Osbert both died attempting to help their uncle William hold on to the bishopric of Durham, and William (the Chancellor) eventually surrendered the see of Durham in exchange for the castle of Richmond, which he then gave to his nephew Richard, it seems more likely that Balfour Paul erred in this instance, and William the Chancellor's brother John was indeed the father of four sons, all nephews to William Comyn.

sourceList.title: JOHN de Comyn, Constable of Scotland, 'MyHeritage Family Trees'

zh.lifeSummary: John de Comyn是Maud Cumton of Morayshire的兒子，於1053年出生在英國蘇格蘭莫雷。他於大約1081年與Sybil de Gifford在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1135年在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾享年82歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭金加迪爾。

variants.name: Dey

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Comyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: John de Comyn was born in 1053, in Moray, Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Maud Cumton of Morayshire. He married Sybil de Gifford about 1081, in Elginshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1135, in Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 82, and was buried in Kincardineshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: John de Comyn est né en 1053, à Moray, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils de Maud Cumton of Morayshire. Il a épousé Sybil de Gifford environ 1081, à Elginshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1135, à Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à Kincardineshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: John de Comyn wurde 1053 in Moray, Schottland, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Maud Cumton of Morayshire geboren. Er hat um 1081 in Elginshire, Scotland Sybil de Gifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1135 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Kincardineshire, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Kincardineshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: John de Comyn è nato il nel 1053 nel Moray, Scozia, Regno Unito come figlio di Maud Cumton of Morayshire. Lui sposato Sybil de Gifford circa 1081, nel Elginshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1135, nel Kincardineshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 82 anni, e fu sepolto a Kincardineshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Comynは1053年にイギリススコットランドモーアイでMaud Cumton of Morayshireの息子として生まれた。約1081年にスコットランドエルジンシュアイアでSybil de Giffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1135年にイギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアで82歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドキンカーディンエシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Comyn(은)는 1053년 영국 스코틀랜드 모라이에서 Maud Cumton of Morayshire의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1081년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르에서 Sybil de Gifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1135년 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 킨카딘실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: John de Comyn nasceu em 1053, em Moray, Escócia, United Kingdom filho de Maud Cumton of Morayshire. Ele casou-se com Sybil de Gifford aproximadamente 1081, em Elginshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1135, em Kincardineshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em Kincardineshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: John de Comyn родился в 1053 г. в местности мореи, Шотландия, Великобритания. Его мать звали Maud Cumton of Morayshire. Он женился на женщине по имени Sybil de Gifford около 1081 г. в местности элгиншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1135 г. в местности кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности кинкардаинешир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: John de Comyn nació en 1053, en Moray, Escocia, Reino Unido como hijo de Maud Cumton of Morayshire. Se casó con Sybil de Gifford aproximadamente 1081, en Elginshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1135, en Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en Kincardineshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
